zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles Cartwright於1820年3月20日出生在美國印第安納州克拉克時，他的父親Noah Cartwright22歲，母親Elizabeth Overman22歲。他於1843年3月30日與Cynthia Ann Ryan在美國印第安納州克拉克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1850年住在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德劍橋大學默里 阿爾班尼且於1870年住在美國印第安納州哈里森傑克遜 堂史普。他去世於1882年2月16日在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德享年61歲，埋葬在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德格林維爾 堂史普格林維爾莫里斯 派利 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles Cartwright于1820年3月20日出生在美国印第安纳州克拉克时，他的父亲Noah Cartwright22岁，母亲Elizabeth Overman22岁。他于1843年3月30日与Cynthia Ann Ryan在美国印第安纳州克拉克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1850年住在美国印第安纳州弗洛伊德劍橋大學默里 阿爾班尼且于1870年住在美国印第安纳州哈里森傑克遜 堂史普。他去世于1882年2月16日在美国印第安纳州弗洛伊德享年61岁，埋葬在美国印第安纳州弗洛伊德格林維爾 堂史普格林維爾莫里斯 派利 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Cartwright
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): occupational name from Middle English cart(e) ‘cart’ + wright(e) ‘craftsman’ (Old English cræt or Old Norse kartr + Old English wyrhta), for a cartwright, a maker of carts.
Variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Gathright
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Cartwright  .

variants.name: Cartright
meanings: Probably also a variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Courtright
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Kortrijk: habitational name from the city of Kortrijk in Flanders, in French known as Courtrai. Compare Cartwright , Cortright , Courtwright , Curtright , Cutright , and Kortright .
Perhaps also an altered form of English   Cartwright  .

History: The progenitors of the (majority of the) Courtrights (and Cortrights, Kortrights, etc.) were the brothers Jan and Michiel Bastiansen van Kortryk from Leerdam in the Netherlands, who arrived with their families to New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1663. The descendants of one or another brother also bear the surnames Kortright, Kortwright, Cortright, Co(u)rtwright, Cartwright, and Cu(r)tright.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

historicEvents.title: Financial Relief for Public Land
historicEvents.description: A United States law to provide financial relief for the purchasers of Public Lands. It permitted the earlier buyers, that couldn't pay completely for the land, to return the land back to the government. This granted them a credit towards the debt they had on land. Congress, also, extended credit to buyer for eight more years. Still while being in economic panic and the shortage of currency made by citizens, the government hoped that with the time extension, the economy would improve.

historicEvents.title: State Capital Moves to Indianapolis
historicEvents.description: The state capital was moved from Corydon to Indianapolis on January 10, 1825.

historicEvents.title: Indiana Nears Bankruptcy
historicEvents.description: The State of Indiana was near bankruptcy in 1841 due to the inability to repay interest incurred for the Massive Internal Improvement Act. The state liquidated much of its public works. Many of the projects were handed over to the state’s creditors as a way to reduce debt. Only two of the eight proposed infrastructure projects were completed by the creditors.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: The Town of Santa Claus
historicEvents.description: The town of Santa Fe was denied their application for a United States Postal Service as a town of Santa Fe, Indiana, was already established. Several meetings were held and the name was changed to Santa Claus, Indiana. The United States Postal Service granted their application. Due to the name, the post office in Santa Claus continues to receive thousands of letters to Santa Claus from children around the world each December.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Fifteenth Amendment
historicEvents.description: Prohibits the federal government and each state from denying a citizen the right to vote based on that citizen's race, color, or previous condition of servitude. It was the last of the Reconstruction Amendments.

historicEvents.title: Giving all the right to vote
historicEvents.description: The Act was an extension of the Fifteenth Amendment, that prohibited discrimination by state offices in voter registration. It also helped empower the President with the authority to enforce the first section of the Fifteenth Amendment throughout the United States. Being the first of three Enforcement Acts passed by the Congress, it helped combat attacks on the suffrage rights of African Americans.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles Cartwright was born on 20 March 1820, in Clark, Indiana, United States, his father, Noah Cartwright, was 22 and his mother, Elizabeth Overman, was 22. He married Cynthia Ann Ryan on 30 March 1843, in Clark, Indiana, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He lived in New Albany, Floyd, Indiana, United States in 1850 and Jackson Township, Harrison, Indiana, United States in 1870. He died on 16 February 1882, in Floyd, Indiana, United States, at the age of 61, and was buried in Morris Family Cemetery, Greenville, Greenville Township, Floyd, Indiana, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles Cartwrightが1820年3月20日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークで生まれた時，父Noah Cartwrightは22歳，母Elizabeth Overmanは22歳であった。1843年3月30日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークでCynthia Ann Ryanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドニュー アルバニーに，1870年にはアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州ハーリソンジャクソン トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1882年2月16日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドで61歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドグレエンヴィル トウンシュイップグレエンヴィルモリス ファミリー セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Greenville Cemetery, Floyd, Indiana,: 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 그린빌 타운시프 그린빌 모리스 파밀리 세멧리
ko.Jackson Township, Harrison, Indiana, Uni: 미국 인디애나 주 해리슨 잭슨 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Charles Cartwright(이)가 1820년 3월 20일 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Noah Cartwright(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Overman(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1843년 3월 30일 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 Cynthia Ann Ryan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 뉴 알바니에서, 1870년도에 미국 인디애나 주 해리슨 잭슨 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1882년 2월 16일 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 그린빌 타운시프 그린빌 모리스 파밀리 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles Cartwright nasceu em 20 de março de 1820, em Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Noah Cartwright, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Overman, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Cynthia Ann Ryan em 30 de março de 1843, em Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele viveu em New Albany, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América em 1850 e em Jackson Township, Harrison, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América em 1870. Ele faleceu em 16 de fevereiro de 1882, em Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Morris Family Cemetery, Greenville, Greenville Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Jackson Township, Harrison, Indiana, Uni: джекксон тоуншип, харрисон, Индиана, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles Cartwright родился 20 марта 1820 г. в местности кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Noah Cartwright было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Overman, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Cynthia Ann Ryan 30 марта 1843 г. в местности кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он жил в местности ню албани, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году и в местности джекксон тоуншип, харрисон, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году. Он умер 16 февраля 1882 г. в местности флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности моррис фамили кеметири, гринвилл, гринвилл тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles Cartwright nació el 20 de marzo de 1820, en Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos, su papá, Noah Cartwright, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Overman, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Cynthia Ann Ryan el 30 de marzo de 1843, en Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Vivió en New Albany, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos en 1850 y en Jackson Township, Harrison, Indiana, Estados Unidos en 1870. Murió el 16 de febrero de 1882, en Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Morris Family Cemetery, Greenville, Greenville Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos.
